name: Eubie Blake
abstract: James Hubert "Eubie" Blake (February 7, 1887 – February 12, 1983) was an American pianist and  composer of ragtime, jazz, and popular music. Blake began his career in 1912, and during World War I he worked in partnership with the singer, drummer, and comedian Broadway Jones.

about_quotes.speaker: Herman Autrey
about_quotes.evidence: I worked under Eubie in pit bands, lots of times. Yeah, he was directing the band, directing the orchestra

about_quotes.speaker: Sol Yaged
about_quotes.evidence: That was written by my dear friend Eubie Blake. ... He was a very close friend of mine, Eubie Blake.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sol Yaged interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I've heard Eubie Blake and Earl Hines talk about learning music by listening...
about_quotes.doc_title: 68088_Wilbur B. Ware, Sr oral history interview

about_quotes.speaker: Sammy Price
about_quotes.evidence: Eubie Blake crowned me King of Boogie-Woogie. He said...when he came in, he says, 'Sammy,' and he put the crown on my head

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Burrell
about_quotes.evidence: From Eubie Blake to Herbie Hancock and all in between... that’s just on the piano, I even played with Teddy Wilson
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Burrell — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Freddie Moore
about_quotes.evidence: I went back again with Eubie Blake. Because Eubie Blake had a fourteen-piece band.
